LanceDB depends on prost-build which requires protoc at build time.
The reusable CI workflow does not install protoc, causing build failures.

Add protobuf-src as a build dependency with a build.rs that sets PROTOC
to the vendored binary, ensuring builds succeed in any environment.

* Add can_be_folded_to_const guard for boolean reduction fast path

When the boolean reduction input is a compile-time constant, the
cast→reduce_min/max→cast chain propagates constness into downstream
operations. This causes MIL's const_elimination pass to aggressively
fold large subgraphs, producing different numerical results from
runtime evaluation. The while-loop fallback naturally breaks this
const chain, so we skip the fast path for constant inputs.

Also changed compute_units to ALL to test if this is a CoreML CPU
backend bug (per maintainer suggestion).

commit.message:
Add DataFlags to subscriptions and unary Get message

Subscribe and Get messages now carry a DataFlags bitmask (Book=0x01,
Info=0x02, All=0x03) so clients choose which data channels to receive.

Wire format changes:
- Subscribe/Get: [header][flags 1B][symbolLen 1B][symbol...]
- SubscribeOk: [header][securityId 8B][flags 1B][symbolLen 1B][symbol...]
- flags=0x00 treated as All for convenience

Behavior:
- Subscribe(flags): snapshot + incremental forwarding filtered by flags
- Get(flags): one-shot snapshot only, no subscription activation
- Incremental events dispatched only to clients whose flags match
  (Book events → DataFlags.Book subscribers, Info → DataFlags.Info)

Fix Worker deploy crash by stubbing unused react-player backends (#22)

`react-player` bundles all player backends via `React.lazy` dynamic
imports, but Vite inlines them into a single SSR chunk evaluated at
Worker startup. Two backends crash at module-evaluation time in the
Workers runtime:

- `@vimeo/player` — `throw new Error("Sorry, the Vimeo Player API is not
available in this browser.")`
- `imsc` (via `dashjs`) —
`window.getComputedStyle(document.documentElement)` at top level

Since this app only uses YouTube, alias all 8 non-YouTube backends to a
no-op component via Vite `resolve.alias`:

```ts
// vite.config.mts
resolve: {
  alias: [
    "dash-video-element/react",
    "hls-video-element/react",
    "vimeo-video-element/react",
    // ... 5 more
  ].map((pkg) => ({
    find: new RegExp(`^${pkg.replace("/", "\\/")}$`),
    replacement: noopPlayerStub,
  })),
},
```

- **`src/stubs/noop-player.ts`** — `export default function NoopPlayer()
{ return null; }`
- **`vite.config.mts`** — resolve aliases for all non-YouTube player
backends

Worker bundle: 3,069 KB → 942 KB. SSR bundle: 3,506 KB → 530 KB.
